description: Business / finops view: live cart-add revenue rate, projected loss when k3s goes down, and the ROI math. Tune the four variables at top (avg cart-add value, outage hours, baseline ATC/hr, annual hours) to drive the numbers.

panels.description: Live revenue pulse — dollars added to carts per minute. Flat ≈ outage.

panels.title: 💰 Revenue right now — what the engine is making
Revenue per hour — right now
panels.description: Cart-add events in the last 1 hour × avg cart-add value. The pulse of the business.

panels.title: Revenue — last 24 hours
panels.description: Total cart-add value in the trailing 24 hours.

panels.title: Annualized run rate
panels.description: Last 24h revenue × 365. What this engine grosses if today repeats every day for a year.

panels.title: Per-minute pulse
panels.description: Smoothed 5-minute revenue rate, expressed per minute. Zero means no carts being added.

panels.title: Avg cart-add value (tunable)
panels.description: Set this to your real average order-line value. Default $150 = midpoint of gift-finder persona budgets.

panels.title: 📉 Missed revenue — what today's downtime actually cost
Missed revenue today (last 24h)
panels.description: Measured shortfall: (`baseline_atc_per_hour` × 24h) − today's actual cart-adds, valued at `avg_atc_value`. Positive = revenue left on the table due to outages, slowdowns, or capacity caps. Negative = you over-performed baseline.

panels.title: Missed this hour
panels.description: Shortfall in the last 60 minutes vs. baseline. Live bleed indicator.

panels.title: Revenue capture rate (24h)
panels.description: Percent of baseline revenue actually captured today. 100% = matched baseline; 70% = lost ~30% of expected revenue; >100% = beat baseline.

panels.title: Expected today (baseline)
panels.description: What the engine should have produced today at baseline rate.

panels.title: Actual today
panels.description: Measured revenue in the trailing 24 hours.

panels.title: 🛑 Projected outage cost — what a future N-hour outage would cost

options.content:
### What this means

When the engine is **healthy**, customers add roughly **${baseline_atc_per_hour} carts an hour**, each worth about **\$${avg_atc_value}**.

That's the revenue you make every hour the lights are on.

When the engine **stops for ${outage_hours} hours**, that hourly revenue evaporates. The red number to the right is the bill.

---

Even a "great" **99 % uptime SLA** still allows **87.6 hours of downtime per year**. At this engine's rate, that's millions of dollars you'll never see.

> **The four boxes at the top of the page tune the math.** Drag them to your own numbers and the whole page updates live.

panels.title: A ${outage_hours}-hour outage costs
panels.description: What an outage of `${outage_hours}` hours, at the healthy cart-add rate of `${baseline_atc_per_hour}/hr` and an average cart value of `\$${avg_atc_value}`, would cost in lost cart-add revenue.

panels.title: Outage length
panels.description: Tune this with the textbox at the top of the page.

panels.title: Healthy rate
panels.description: What the engine adds to carts every hour when everything's working. Tune at the top.

panels.title: Bleeding rate (per hour down)
panels.description: What you lose for every hour the engine is down.

panels.title: Annual cost of a 99% SLA
panels.description: A 99 % uptime SLA still allows 87.6 hours of downtime per year. This is what those hours cost.

panels.title: 📈 When the engine ran vs. stopped
k3s node Ready — green = engine running, red = revenue stopped
panels.description: Each row is a node. Color = Ready status. Red gaps = no scheduling → loadgen halts → ATC stops → revenue stops.

panels.title: Customer journey health — can shoppers reach checkout?
panels.description: 1 = every component a customer needs is up (k3s nodes Ready, k6 driving traffic, neoncart serving the website, llm-gateway handling chats). Any one of those breaking drops this to 0 — customers either can't reach the site or hit an error before checkout. This is the same signal that turns 'Lost revenue' on in the chart below.

targets.expr: clamp_max(min(kube_node_status_condition{cluster="k3s",condition="Ready",status="true"}) * clamp_max((count(kube_pod_status_phase{namespace="observibelity",pod=~"k6-traffic-.*",phase="Running"} == 1) or vector(0)), 1) * clamp_max((count(kube_pod_status_phase{namespace="observibelity",pod=~"neoncart-.*",phase="Running"} == 1) or vector(0)), 1) * clamp_max((count(kube_pod_status_phase{namespace="observibelity",pod=~"llm-gateway-.*",phase="Running"} == 1) or vector(0)), 1), 1)

panels.title: 📊 Revenue per 10-minute block — what we made, what we missed
What we made — per 10-minute block, stacked by model
panels.description: Each bar = total cart-add revenue in a 10-minute window, broken down by which model drove the conversation. Hover a bar to see the model mix. Legend on the right shows trailing 24 h totals.

panels.title: Lost revenue — gated by k3s / critical-pod errors
panels.description: Turns ON when an error is firing on k3s or a critical pod (k6-traffic, neoncart, llm-gateway) and stays ON until the error clears. Value per 10-min bar = expected revenue × fraction of the window the error was firing. Yin/yang with the 'What we made' chart above — when one is green, the other should be empty.

panels.title: Revenue mix by model — last 24h
panels.description: Which models drove cart-add events. Higher = more revenue contribution.

panels.title: Top products by revenue — last 24h
panels.description: Top 10 SKUs that ended up in carts.

panels.title: 💡 Why this dashboard pays for itself

options.content:
### The math, for finance

| Metric | Formula | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| **Annual run rate** | `(24-hour revenue) × 365` | What this AI workload contributes to the top line at today's pace |
| **Loss rate** | `Baseline ATC/hr × Avg cart-add value` | The bleed rate the moment the engine stops |
| **Cost of a single outage** | `Loss rate × Outage hours` | The headline number above |
| **Cost of 99 % uptime SLA** | `Loss rate × 87.6 hr` | The annual cost of a "great" 99 % SLA. Most platforms target 99.9 % (8.76 hr) or 99.99 % (52 min). |
| **Observability ROI** | `(Cost of outages prevented) / (Annual platform cost)` | If avoiding one 3-hour outage pays for the year, the platform is profit. |

> **The pitch.** The dashboard above shows the engine in production. Every flat spot in the green bars is a moment we made nothing. Observability is what turns those flat spots from "noticed in next week's QBR" into "paged in 90 seconds." Multiply by the loss rate to see what each minute of MTTR is worth.

title: AI Obs — Cost of an Outage (live revenue × downtime impact)
